primary_accounting_issue: Whether foreign exchange risk arising from intragroup dividend receivables can be designated as a hedged item under IFRS hedge accounting requirements

primary_authority.reason: Defines eligible hedged items and explicitly limits hedge accounting to transactions with parties external to the reporting entity
Addresses treatment of intragroup monetary items and exchange differences in consolidated financial statements

supporting_authority.reason: Provides specific guidance on hedge accounting for net investments in foreign operations and the exception for intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements

peripheral_authority.reason: Disclosures about hedge accounting and financial instruments but does not govern eligibility for hedge accounting designation
Addresses presentation and classification of financial instruments but not hedge accounting eligibility
Disclosures about structured entities and restrictions, not directly relevant to hedge accounting designation for intragroup items

treatment_families.family: Designation of intragroup monetary items as hedged items in consolidated financial statements
Ineligibility of internal group transactions as hedged items under general hedge accounting rules
Recognition of foreign exchange gains and losses on monetary intragroup items

approaches.label: Net investment hedge exception applies
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 paragraph 6 permits hedge accounting for foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements when the intragroup item creates exposure to foreign exchange gains or losses not fully eliminated on consolidation

approaches.label: No hedge accounting eligibility for intragroup dividend receivable
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 restricts hedged items to transactions with external parties, and intragroup dividends do not qualify as net investment hedges under IFRIC 16 unless the dividend receivable is part of net assets of a foreign operation

approaches.label: Foreign exchange recognition in profit or loss without hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 paragraph 45 requires recognition of exchange differences on intragroup monetary items in profit or loss (or OCI for certain circumstances) even when not designated as hedged items
